title: Settings

skillsHub.title: Skills Hub
skillsHub.description: Centrally manage AI agent skills. Install once, use across all assistants.
skillsHub.discoveryAlert: Detected skills from your CLI tools. Import them to use in Wayland.
skillsHub.noSkills: No skills found. Import some to get started.
skillsHub.tipContent: Skills configured here can be enabled for any assistant in the "Assistants" setup page; skills are imported and exported using symbolic links.
skillsHub.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
skillsHub.recommendSuccess: Recommended successfully: {{count}} skills selected based on rules
skillsHub.noRecommendations: No matching skills found, please select manually
skillsHub.exportAlreadyExists: Skill already exists in the target directory

claudeYoloModeDesc: Bypass all Claude Code permission checks (equivalent to --dangerously-skip-permissions).
customCssDesc: Enter custom CSS styles here to modify the interface appearance. The system will automatically add !important to ensure highest priority. Changes will take effect immediately.
pdfToPptAssistantDesc: Convert PDF to PPT with watermark removal rules.
game3dAssistantDesc: Generate a complete 3D platform collection game in one HTML file.
assistantDescriptionPlaceholder: What can this assistant help with?
assistantsListDescription: Build task-specific assistants by combining an AI agent with custom rules and skills.
searchAssistants: Search assistants by name or description
assistantNoMatch: No assistants match the current filters.
assistantPromptHint: Enter rules for this assistant. Note: Gemini currently only supports rules.
assistantRulesHint: Define additional rules for assistant behavior.
assistantModelsHint: Select which models this assistant can use.
assistantSkillsHint: List preferred tools/skills for the assistant.
deleteAssistantConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this assistant? This action cannot be undone.
skillPathPlaceholder: Enter or browse skill folder path(s), separated by comma
pleaseSelectSkillPath: Please select a skill folder path
deletePendingSkillConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove "{{name}}"? This skill has not been imported yet.
deletePendingSkillNote: This will only remove the skill from the pending list. You can add it again using the "Add Skills" feature.
removeCustomSkillConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove "{{name}}" from this assistant?
removeCustomSkillNote: This will only remove the skill from this assistant. The skill will remain in the builtin skills and can be added back later.
delete_preset_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this preset?

cssTheme.selectOrCustomize: Select preset theme or custom CSS
cssTheme.name: Name
cssTheme.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this theme? This action cannot be undone.

autoPreviewOfficeFilesDesc: Automatically open newly created Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files in the current workspace. Office temporary lock files are ignored.
startOnBootDesc: Launch Wayland automatically when you sign in to macOS, Windows, or Linux.
startOnBootUnsupported: Available only in packaged macOS, Windows, and Linux apps.
startOnBootUpdateFailed: Failed to update start on boot setting
agentIdleTimeoutDesc: Kill idle agent processes after this duration to free memory
systemUpdateChannelUnavailable: Update channel unavailable: {{error}}. Auto-updates are disabled until next launch.
deleteModelConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this model?
deleteAllModelConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all models?
pleaseEnterBaseUrlAndApiKey: Please enter base url and api key
multiApiKeyTip: 💡 To add multiple API Keys for auto-rotation, configure in platform edit later
multiApiKeyEditTip: Support multiple API Keys, one per line, system will auto-rotate
apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter API Key(s), one per line for multiple keys
customModelSupportNote: Note: Wayland Core and Gemini CLI Agent currently support custom models.
addModelHint: Pick or type a model ID and it will be appended to this provider.
addModelNoExisting: No models have been added for this provider yet. They will appear here once added.
addModelTips: You can paste any model name directly. Keep it consistent with the provider console for best compatibility.
modelProtocolTip: Choose the API request format for this model. new-api gateway will route to the upstream using the selected protocol.

bedrock.regionHint: Select the AWS region for Bedrock models
bedrock.connectionSuccess: Connection successful! AWS credentials are valid.
bedrock.connectionFailed: Connection failed. Please check your credentials.
bedrock.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields first.

restartConfirm: Changes will restart the application. Continue?
fontSizeDescription: Adjust the UI zoom level so the entire interface matches your preferred size.

telegram.help: Chat with your Wayland AI assistant via Telegram. Create a bot with @BotFather to get started.

slack.comingSoonBody: Bot integration via Socket Mode. Post and receive messages in any channel of your workspace.
slack.help: Native Slack bot integration via @slack/bolt. Choose Socket Mode for the simplest setup (no public webhook URL required) or Events API if you already have a stable HTTPS endpoint.
slack.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Slack workspace will replace your existing one.

transport.label: Transport
transport.help: Socket Mode opens an outbound WebSocket Slack initiates — no public URL needed. Events API uses an inbound HTTPS webhook verified with a signing secret.

botToken.label: Bot Token
botToken.help: OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token. Starts with "xoxb-".

appToken.label: App-Level Token
appToken.help: Required for Socket Mode. Basic Information → App-Level Tokens → Generate Token and Scopes with connections:write. Starts with "xapp-".
appToken.required: Socket Mode requires an app-level token

signingSecret.label: Signing Secret
signingSecret.help: Required for Events API. Basic Information → App Credentials → Signing Secret. Used to verify the X-Slack-Signature HMAC on every webhook delivery.
signingSecret.required: Events API transport requires a signing secret

discord.comingSoonBody: Bot integration via the Discord Gateway. Servers, threads, DMs, slash commands.
discord.help: Native Discord bot integration via the Gateway. Create a Discord application and bot in the Developer Portal, then paste the bot token below.
discord.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Discord bot will replace your existing one.

botToken.label: Bot Token
botToken.help: Find this in the Discord Developer Portal under your application → Bot → Reset Token.

applicationId.label: Application ID
applicationId.help: Optional. Required only when you register slash commands for this bot.

publicKey.label: Public Key
publicKey.help: Optional. Required only if you later switch to the HTTP interaction endpoint instead of the Gateway.

webhook.comingSoonBody: Generic inbound HTTP endpoint with HMAC signature verification. Use for custom integrations.

dingtalk.help: Enterprise messaging via DingTalk bots. Create a custom bot inside a DingTalk group to get the access token.

lark.help: Workspace messaging for Lark / Feishu groups. Create a custom app in the Lark developer console to get App ID and App Secret.

wechat.help: QR-based pairing with WeChat personal accounts. Scan the pairing QR with WeChat to enable conversation routing.

wecom.help: Enterprise WeChat for company workspaces. Register a self-built application to get Corp ID, Agent ID, and Secret.

whatsapp.comingSoonBody: Meta WhatsApp Business Cloud API. Inbound via verified webhooks, outbound REST. 24-hour customer service window applies to free-form replies.
whatsapp.help: Three backends: Baileys (direct WhatsApp Web protocol, recommended), WhatsApp Web.js (alternate Puppeteer-based), or Meta Business Cloud API (verified business numbers).
whatsapp.replaceWarning: Connecting a new WhatsApp account will replace your existing one.
whatsapp.accountLockWarning: Connecting a new WhatsApp account will replace your existing one. Existing QR pairing or webhook URL becomes invalid.

backend.label: Backend
backend.help: Baileys is recommended for personal accounts. Meta Business is required for verified business accounts and templated messaging.

accessToken.label: Access Token
accessToken.help: Meta system-user or temporary access token. Generated in Meta Business Suite → System Users.
accessToken.required: Access Token and Phone Number ID are required for Meta backend

phoneNumberId.label: Phone Number ID
phoneNumberId.help: Numeric ID from Meta Business → WhatsApp → Phone Numbers. Not the phone number itself.
phoneNumberId.required: Phone Number ID is required for Meta backend

businessAccountId.label: Business Account ID
businessAccountId.help: Optional. Enables richer metadata in inbound message events.

verifyToken.label: Verify Token
verifyToken.help: Operator-chosen string Meta echoes during the GET /webhook handshake. Must match what you paste into the Meta dashboard.

qrPairing.title: QR Pairing
qrPairing.help: Open WhatsApp on your phone → Settings → Linked Devices → Link a Device, then scan the code below. The code rotates every minute until paired.
qrPairing.refreshing: Waiting for pairing code from bridge…

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into Meta Business → WhatsApp → Configuration → Webhook callback URL.

smsTwilio.comingSoonBody: Send and receive SMS via Twilio Programmable Messaging. Per-number webhook routing with HMAC-SHA1 signature verification. US deployments require A2P 10DLC registration.
smsTwilio.accountLockWarning: Connecting a new SMS account will replace your existing one. Existing webhook URL becomes invalid.
smsTwilio.a2pNotice: US deployments require A2P 10DLC brand and campaign registration. Toll-free numbers require separate verification. Both processes can take several days.

accountSid.label: Account SID
accountSid.help: Find your Account SID in the Twilio Console dashboard. Starts with "AC".

authToken.label: Auth Token
authToken.help: Treat the auth token like a password — Twilio signs every webhook with it.

fromNumber.label: From Number
fromNumber.help: E.164 format, e.g. +14155550123. Provide either a From Number or a Messaging Service SID.

messagingServiceSid.label: Messaging Service SID
messagingServiceSid.help: Optional alternative to From Number. Use a Messaging Service to enable number pooling or sender ID rotation.

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into the Twilio number "A Message Comes In" webhook field (POST).

signal.comingSoonBody: Connect via signal-cli subprocess. End-to-end encrypted. Requires a dedicated phone number for one-time registration.
signal.help: End-to-end encrypted messaging via signal-cli subprocess daemon. Requires a dedicated phone number registered with Signal. signal-cli must be installed or available via the bundled runtime.
signal.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Signal account will replace your existing one.
signal.installHowTo: signal-cli must be installed separately if the bundled runtime is not present. Install via: brew install signal-cli | apt-get install signal-cli. After installing, register your number: signal-cli -a +1… register

phoneNumber.label: Phone Number
phoneNumber.help: E.164 format — must be registered with Signal. A dedicated number is recommended.
phoneNumber.required: Please enter your Signal phone number
phoneNumber.invalid: Phone number must be in E.164 format, e.g. +14155551234

cliPath.label: signal-cli path (optional)
cliPath.help: Leave blank to use the bundled runtime or the signal-cli on your PATH.

matrix.comingSoonBody: Federation-aware via matrix-js-sdk. Optional E2E encryption with device verification. Compatible with any homeserver.
matrix.help: Federation-aware messaging via matrix-js-sdk. Provide your homeserver URL, full mxid (e.g. @bot:matrix.org), and a long-lived access token.
matrix.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Matrix account will replace your existing one.
matrix.accessTokenHowTo: How to get an access token: see https://t2bot.io/docs/access_tokens/ or your homeserver settings.

homeserverUrl.label: Homeserver URL
homeserverUrl.help: HTTPS base URL of your Matrix homeserver. Use https://matrix.org for the public flagship server.

userId.label: User ID (mxid)
userId.help: Full Matrix ID including the server suffix, e.g. @bot:matrix.org.
userId.required: Please enter a Matrix user ID (mxid)

accessToken.label: Access Token
accessToken.help: Long-lived access token. Element: All settings → Help & About → Access Token. Or use a dedicated bot account.

msTeams.comingSoonBody: Microsoft Bot Framework via Azure Bot Service. Adaptive Cards for rich messages. Requires Azure subscription and Teams admin approval.
msTeams.help: Bot Framework via Azure Bot Service. Register a bot, set the webhook endpoint, and paste your App ID and client secret below.
msTeams.replaceWarning: Saving new credentials will replace the existing MS Teams bot connection.
msTeams.setupHint: Register your bot in the Azure Portal: Azure Bot Service → Create resource → configure Microsoft Teams channel → set messaging endpoint to your Wayland webhook URL → copy App ID and client secret here.

appId.label: App ID (MicrosoftAppId)
appId.help: Azure AD app registration client ID — found in Azure Portal → App Registrations → Overview.

appPassword.label: App Password (Client Secret)
appPassword.help: Azure AD client secret — App Registrations → Certificates & secrets → New client secret.

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Set this as the Messaging Endpoint in Azure Bot Service.

credentials.bothRequired: Channel Access Token and Channel Secret are required

channelAccessToken.label: Channel Access Token
channelAccessToken.help: Long-lived token from LINE Developers Console → Messaging API → Channel access token.

channelSecret.label: Channel Secret
channelSecret.help: Channel secret from LINE Developers Console → Basic settings. Used to verify webhook signatures.

line.accountLockWarning: Saving new credentials will replace the existing LINE bot connection.

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into LINE Developers Console → Messaging API → Webhook URL.

imessage.comingSoonBody: macOS-only, experimental. AppleScript bridge to Messages.app. Apple silently rate-limits scripted sends so messaging is throttled.

viber.comingSoonBody: Bot integration via the Viber Bot API. Webhook for inbound, REST for outbound. Public URL required.

bluebubbles.comingSoonBody: iMessage via the BlueBubbles relay server. Requires a spare Mac on your network running the BlueBubbles server.

threema.comingSoonBody: Threema Gateway API. B2B contract with per-message pricing. End-to-end encrypted.

mastodon.comingSoonBody: OAuth per-instance plus WebSocket streaming. One credential set per account on one instance.

bluesky.comingSoonBody: AT Protocol via @atproto/api. Jetstream firehose for mentions; XRPC for posting. Use an app password, not your main login.

reddit.comingSoonBody: Reddit API for direct messages. Polling-only (no streaming endpoint for DMs).

twitch.comingSoonBody: Twitch chat via the IRC bridge or EventSub. Connects to one channel per token.
twitch.help: Twitch chat via IRC (tmi.js). Connect a bot account with OAuth to read and send messages in one or more channels.
twitch.connectionFailed: Twitch connection failed — check token and username
twitch.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Twitch bot will replace your existing credentials.
twitch.tokenHowTo: Use a dedicated bot account — never use your personal Twitch account. Generate a token at twitchtokengenerator.com and select the chat:read and chat:edit scopes.

botUsername.label: Bot Username
botUsername.help: The Twitch login name of your bot account (lowercase).

oauthToken.label: OAuth Token
oauthToken.help: Bot OAuth token with chat:read + chat:edit scopes. Get one at twitchtokengenerator.com.

channels.label: Channels
channels.help: Comma-separated channel names to join (e.g. mychannel, otherchannel). The # prefix is optional.
channels.required: Please enter at least one channel to join

nostr.comingSoonBody: Encrypted DMs over Nostr relays. Bring your own private key (nsec).

telegramUserbot.comingSoonBody: MTProto user-mode (gramjs / mtcute). Separate from the Bot API. WARNING — running a userbot can get the phone number banned for ToS violation.

mattermost.comingSoonBody: Self-hostable Slack alternative. WebSocket events plus REST.

googleChat.comingSoonBody: Google Workspace integration via the Chat API. OAuth required.
googleChat.help: Google Chat (Workspace) bot. Paste your service-account JSON keyfile and provide the expected JWT audience (project number or app URL) for webhook verification.
googleChat.replaceWarning: Saving new credentials will replace the existing Google Chat bot connection.
googleChat.enableFailed: Failed to enable Google Chat plugin

serviceAccountJson.label: Service Account JSON
serviceAccountJson.help: Paste the full contents of your Google Cloud service-account keyfile (JSON). The bot must have the Chat API scope (chat.bot).

audience.label: JWT Audience
audience.help: The expected `aud` claim in Google Chat's Bearer JWT. Use your Google Cloud project number (e.g. 123456789012) or the registered app URL.
audience.required: JWT audience is required for webhook verification

credentials.bothRequired: Service Account JSON and JWT audience are required

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into Google Cloud Console → Google Chat API → Configuration → App URL.

nextcloudTalk.comingSoonBody: Self-hosted chat for Nextcloud instances. Username + app password.
nextcloudTalk.help: Self-hosted Nextcloud Talk via app-password Basic auth. Provide your server URL, Nextcloud username, and an app password generated under Personal → Security → App passwords.
nextcloudTalk.replaceWarning: Connecting a new account will replace your existing Nextcloud Talk connection.
nextcloudTalk.appPasswordHowTo: How to create an app password: open Nextcloud → top-right avatar → Personal settings → Security → App passwords.

serverUrl.label: Server URL
serverUrl.help: Base URL of your Nextcloud instance, e.g. https://cloud.example.com
serverUrl.required: Please enter the Nextcloud server URL

appPassword.label: App Password
appPassword.help: Generate under Personal → Security → App passwords in your Nextcloud settings.

synologyChat.comingSoonBody: Chat on Synology NAS. Webhook for inbound, REST for outbound.
synologyChat.replaceWarning: Connecting a new Synology Chat account will replace your existing one.
synologyChat.enableFailed: Failed to enable Synology Chat plugin
synologyChat.howTo: Setup guide: Synology Chat → Administration → Integrations → Incoming/Outgoing Webhooks.

incomingUrl.label: Incoming Webhook URL
incomingUrl.help: The webhook URL from Synology Chat admin panel (Integration → Incoming Webhooks). We POST replies here.
incomingUrl.required: Please enter the Synology Chat incoming webhook URL

incomingToken.label: Webhook Token
incomingToken.help: The token Synology Chat sends with each outgoing webhook request (Outgoing Webhooks → Token). Used to verify inbound deliveries.

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into Synology Chat → Outgoing Webhooks → Outgoing URL.

irc.comingSoonBody: Classic IRC connectivity. Server, port, channels, optional SASL authentication.

qq.comingSoonBody: Tencent QQ messaging. Limited utility outside China.

xDms.premiumGate: Premium API tier required ($200/month). Subscribe to X Developer Platform Basic or higher to enable DMs. The X Basic tier is closed to new signups as of 2026.

emailAgentMail.help: Webhook-driven SaaS email via AgentMail. Each agent gets a dedicated inbox; AgentMail handles deliverability, threading, and signed inbound webhooks.
emailAgentMail.replaceWarning: Replacing the AgentMail account invalidates the existing webhook URL.
emailAgentMail.accountLockWarning: Connecting a new AgentMail account will replace your existing one. Existing webhook URL becomes invalid.

apiKey.label: API Key
apiKey.help: AgentMail dashboard → API Keys. Treat like a password.

inboxAddress.label: Inbox Address
inboxAddress.help: The dedicated inbox AgentMail provisioned for this agent, e.g. agent@workspace.agentmail.to.

webhookSecret.label: Webhook Secret
webhookSecret.help: Optional HMAC secret AgentMail uses to sign webhook deliveries. Copy from the AgentMail dashboard.
webhookSecret.required: Webhook secret is required for signed delivery verification

webhookUrl.label: Inbound Webhook URL
webhookUrl.help: Paste this URL into the AgentMail dashboard "Webhook URL" field.

emailImap.comingSoonBody: Bring your own inbox. IMAP IDLE for inbound, SMTP for outbound. OAuth supported for Gmail and Outlook.
emailImap.help: Bring your own inbox. IMAP IDLE for inbound when available (polling fallback at 30s), SMTP for outbound. Use an app password for Gmail / Outlook.

imapHost.label: IMAP Host
imapHost.help: Hostname of your IMAP server, e.g. imap.gmail.com.

imapPassword.label: IMAP Password
imapPassword.help: Use an app password for Gmail / Outlook — your account login will not work.

useSameAuth.label: Use IMAP credentials for SMTP
useSameAuth.help: When on, SMTP reuses the IMAP user + password. Turn off to supply a separate SMTP login.

smtpHost.label: SMTP Host
smtpHost.help: Leave blank to reuse the IMAP host.

smtpPort.label: SMTP Port
smtpPort.help: Default 587 for STARTTLS. Use 465 for implicit TLS.

smtpTls.label: SMTP TLS
smtpTls.help: STARTTLS when port = 587, implicit TLS when port = 465.

webui.description: Use Wayland as your "24/7 Remote Assistant" - arrange tasks from any remote device, anytime, anywhere.
webui.steps: Step 1. Turn on WebUI; Step 2. Copy the access URL; Step 3. Open in remote browser
webui.enableDesc: Start the web service to allow browser access
webui.allowRemoteDesc: Use remote software/server for secure remote access
webui.qrLoginHint: Scan the QR code with your phone to log in automatically on mobile browser
webui.initialPasswordDesc: Password generated on first startup, please change it
webui.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
webui.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters long
webui.passwordTooLong: Password must be less than 128 characters long
webui.passwordTooCommon: Password is too common, please choose a stronger one
webui.usernameMinLength: Username must be at least 3 characters
webui.usernameMaxLength: Username must be 32 characters or less
webui.usernameFormatError: Use letters, numbers, hyphens, or underscores only
webui.usernameEdgeError: Username cannot start or end with a hyphen or underscore
webui.resetPasswordTooltip: Forgot password? Click to set a new one (no current password required)
webui.resetPasswordHint: Forgot your password? Click "Reset" to generate a new random password
webui.passwordResetSuccess: Password reset. New password is shown above
webui.passwordResetAndCopied: Password reset and copied to clipboard
webui.passwordResetAndFilled: Password reset and filled in form, please save
webui.passwordHint: Password is shown in console on first startup. Use "Change Password" to reset
webui.checkConsoleForPassword: Check application console for initial password
webui.browserNotSupported: WebUI settings not available in browser
webui.browserNotSupportedDesc: For security reasons, WebUI settings can only be managed in the desktop application. Please open the settings panel in Wayland desktop app to configure.
webui.passwordOnlyFirstTime: Password is only shown on first startup and will be hidden afterwards
webui.resetPasswordWarning: Please keep the reset password safe!
webui.featureRemoteDesc: Enable WebUI so your phone or remote browser can access Wayland.
webui.featureChannelsDesc: Connect Telegram, Lark, DingTalk, and WeChat to interact with Wayland from IM apps.
webui.pairedDevices.title: Paired Devices
webui.pairedDevices.revokeConfirm: This will immediately invalidate the session for this device. The device will need to log in again.
webui.activityLog.title: Recent Activity
assistant.botTokenDesc: Open Telegram, find @BotFather and send /newbot to get your Bot Token.
assistant.enableBotDesc: Start the Telegram bot to receive messages
assistant.tokenLocked: Please close the Channel and delete all authorized users before modifying the configuration
assistant.defaultModelDesc: Model used for Telegram conversations. Default uses GEMINI CLI as Agent
assistant.step1: Open Telegram and search for your bot
assistant.step2: Send any message or click /start to initiate pairing
assistant.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
assistant.step4: Once approved, you can start chatting with Gemini through Telegram!
channels.title: Channels
channels.telegramDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via Telegram
channels.slackDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via Slack
channels.discordDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via Discord
channels.larkDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via Lark
channels.dingtalkDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via DingTalk
channels.weixinDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via WeChat
channels.wecomDesc: Chat with Wayland assistant via WeCom (Enterprise WeChat AI Bot)
channels.guide: Connect Telegram, Lark, DingTalk and more to control your always-on Wayland AI agent from any chat app.
channels.selectFirst: Select a channel and configure credentials.
channels.enableAfterConfig: Enable the channel and start chatting with your AI agent.
channels.discord.comingSoonBody: Native Discord bot integration is under development. Configure your bot token, Application ID, and allowed servers once available.
channels.lark.comingSoonBody: Native Lark and Feishu bot integration is under development. Domain picker, AppId/Secret, and advanced webhook options will be available once shipped.
channels.dingtalk.comingSoonBody: Native DingTalk bot integration is under development. ClientId/Secret and stream mode will be available once shipped.
channels.wechat.comingSoonBody: WeChat QR pairing integration is under development. Scan with WeChat to connect once available.
channels.wecom.comingSoonBody: WeCom (Enterprise WeChat) bot integration is under development. Webhook and WebSocket modes with callback URL configuration will be available once shipped.
channels.mediaPathNotAllowed: Couldn't send {{name}} because the file is outside the allowed send directories
imageGenerationDescription: Choose which configured image-capable model the tool should use by default.
speechToTextDescription: Configure the voice input provider used by the chat box on desktop and WebUI.
textToSpeechDescription: Read AI responses aloud using a local or system voice engine.
noAvailable: No available image models, please configure first.
mcpDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this MCP server?
mcpImportPlaceholder: Copy the JSON configuration from the MCP service introduction page (preferably NPX or UVX configuration) and paste it into the input box below.
mcpImportTip1: Use a valid JSON object with a top-level "mcpServers" field.
mcpImportTip2: Each server should define either "command" (stdio) or "url" (http/sse).
mcpImportTip3: Multiple servers can be imported at once.
mcpImportDescription: Wayland will automatically retrieve the MCP installed in your CLI Agent and import it with one click
mcpSyncFailedNoAgents: No available agents detected, unable to sync MCP configuration
mcpDeletedWithCleanup: MCP server deleted and configuration cleaned up

cdp.title: CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol)
cdp.enableDesc: Expose a local CDP endpoint for browser debugging and MCP integration.
cdp.mcpConfigHint: Paste this into your MCP client config to connect to local Chrome DevTools.
cdp.restartRequired: CDP startup setting changed. Restart is required to apply.
cdp.playwrightMcpConfigHint: Use Playwright MCP to connect via CDP endpoint, with accessibility snapshots and browser automation.

needHelpConfigGuide: Need help? Check out the detailed
needHelpTooltip: If you need help, please check
googleCloudProjectPlaceholder: Enter your Google Cloud Project ID
appDescription: Free, local, open-source GUI app for Gemini CLI
bugReportModulePlaceholder: Select the module related to the issue
bugReportDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the issue in detail, including steps to reproduce
bugReportScreenshotDropzoneText: Click, drag, or paste screenshots here
bugReportAutoInfo: System info and logs from the last 3 days will be automatically attached to help us locate the issue faster
bugReportSuccess: Report submitted, thank you for your feedback
bugReportError: Submission failed, please try again later
bugReportModuleSkillsDescription: For issues with the Skills Market, built-in or external skills, plugin install and enable flows, load failures, or plugin execution.
bugReportModuleWebuiDescription: For issues with WebUI startup, remote access, QR login, account or password changes, or browser-side behavior.
bugReportModuleMcpDescription: For issues with MCP server config, OAuth login, tool sync, missing tools, or tool invocation failures.
bugReportModuleChannelDescription: For issues with Telegram, Slack, Lark and other channel plugins, including config, pairing, authorization, or message delivery.
bugReportModuleChatDescription: For issues while sending messages, receiving agent replies, handling context, using the command queue, or other in-chat behavior.
bugReportModuleScheduledTaskDescription: For issues with creating, editing, triggering, reminding, or executing scheduled tasks.
bugReportModuleLlmConfigDescription: For issues with model providers, API keys, login and authentication, rate limits, quotas, or model selection.
bugReportModuleAssistantDescription: For issues with assistant cards, presets, switching defaults, or assistant behavior settings.
bugReportModulePermissionDescription: For issues where local or remote agents are not detected, unavailable, unhealthy, or fail to connect or complete a handshake.
bugReportModuleSessionDescription: For issues with search, history, rename, pin, delete, or how conversation lists are displayed.
bugReportModuleWorkspaceDescription: For issues with workspaces, uploads, folder selection, Office files, preview panels, or diff views.
bugReportModuleAgentTeamDescription: For issues with team creation, leader or teammate coordination, task dispatch, or team message flow.
bugReportModuleDisplaySettingsDescriptio: For issues with theme, scaling, UI styles, or window display.
bugReportModuleSystemSettingsDescription: For issues with system permissions, launch at startup, notifications, storage paths, or platform integration.
bugReportModuleOtherDescription: Use this when the issue spans multiple modules, or when you are not sure which category fits best.
deleteCustomAgentConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this custom agent?
customAcpAgentJsonDescription: Paste your custom agent configuration JSON below
customAcpAgentTip2: name: Display name shown in the agent selector
noCliDetected: No CLI tools detected. Please install an ACP-compatible CLI first.
agentNamePlaceholder: Enter a name for this agent
commandHelp: The executable command to run the agent CLI
argsHelp: Space-separated arguments passed to the command
testConnectionSuccess: Connection successful! CLI exists and ACP protocol is working.
testConnectionFailCli: Command not found. Make sure it is installed and in your PATH.
testConnectionFailAcp: CLI found but ACP initialization failed.
simpleModeTip: Select a detected CLI and give it a custom name. Switch to Advanced mode for environment variables and other options.
agentModelSettingsTooltip: Configure default models for each AI agent. Models will be injected when starting new conversations.
agentModelNote: Note: Model changes will take effect in new conversations.
agentModelCustomNote: You can also enter any custom model ID supported by the agent.
protocolOpenAICompatible: Detected OpenAI-compatible protocol, configuration is correct
protocolSwitchSuggestion: Detected {{protocol}} protocol, consider switching to {{platform}} for better support
protocolTimeout: Connection timeout, please check network or API URL
protocolInvalidKey: Invalid API Key, please check your key
protocolCheckConfig: Unable to identify API protocol, please check configuration
packyapiOpenAIDetected: PackyAPI: Detected OpenAI format. For Claude format, remove /v1 from URL and select Anthropic platform
packyapiAnthropicDetected: PackyAPI: Detected Claude format. For OpenAI format, add /v1 to URL and select OpenAI/Custom platform
noSkillsFound: No valid skills found in the selected path(s)

assistant.botTokenDesc: Open Telegram, find @BotFather and send /newbot to get your Bot Token.
assistant.step1: Open Telegram and search for your bot
assistant.step2: Send any message or click /start to initiate pairing
assistant.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
assistant.step4: Once approved, you can start chatting with AI through Telegram!
assistant.autoFollowCliModel: Automatically follow the model when CLI is running

lark.encryptKeyDesc: Optional: For event encryption (from Event Subscription settings)
lark.verificationTokenDesc: Optional: For event verification (from Event Subscription settings)
lark.credentialsRequired: Please enter App ID and App Secret
lark.credentialsSaved: Credentials already configured. Enter new values to update.
lark.step1: Open Feishu/Lark and find your bot application
lark.step2: Send any message to initiate pairing
lark.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
lark.step4: Once approved, you can start chatting with the AI assistant through Lark!
lark.waitingConnection: WebSocket connection is being established. Please wait...
lark.autoFollowCliModel: Automatically follow the model when CLI is running

dingtalk.credentialsRequired: Please enter Client ID and Client Secret
dingtalk.credentialsSaved: Credentials already configured. Enter new values to update.
dingtalk.step1: Open DingTalk and find your bot application
dingtalk.step2: Send any message to initiate pairing
dingtalk.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
dingtalk.step4: Once approved, you can start chatting with the AI assistant through DingTalk!
dingtalk.waitingConnection: Connection is being established. Please wait...

weixin.scanPrompt: Please scan the QR code with WeChat
weixin.loginExpired: QR code expired, please try again
weixin.step1: Find and send a message to your bot in WeChat
weixin.step2: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
weixin.step3: Once approved, you can start chatting with the AI assistant through WeChat!
weixin.loginRequired: Please login with WeChat QR code first

wecom.callbackHint: Production usually requires a public HTTPS URL reachable by WeCom. Start WebUI first; use a reverse proxy or tunnel if needed.
wecom.tokenDesc: Must match the Token in the WeCom bot callback configuration
wecom.encodingAesKeyDesc: 43-character key from WeCom (AES encryption for callbacks)
wecom.credentialsSaved: Credentials already configured. Enter new values to update.
wecom.configureFirst: Please save Token and EncodingAESKey first
wecom.step1: Configure the callback URL in WeCom admin to match your WebUI base + /channels/wecom/webhook
wecom.step2: Send any message to the bot in WeCom to start pairing
wecom.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
wecom.step4: Once approved, you can chat with the AI assistant through WeCom.
wecom.waitingConnection: Ensure WebUI is running so WeCom can reach the callback URL.

extensionAssistantReadonly: Extension assistants are read-only. You can duplicate it and edit the copy.

agentManagement.localAgentsDescription: Gemini CLI is built-in. Other agents are auto-detected after local installation.
agentManagement.remoteAgentsDescription: Only remote OpenClaw connections are supported for now. Other agents are in development.
agentManagement.marketEmpty: No installable agents found. Check your network connection and make sure GitHub is reachable.
agentManagement.marketContributionHint: Want a new Agent listed here?
agentManagement.customEmpty: No custom agents configured. Click "Add Custom Agent" to create one.

remoteAgent.title: Remote Agents
remoteAgent.empty: No remote agents configured. Click "Add" to connect to a remote agent service.
remoteAgent.name: Name
remoteAgent.allowInsecureHint: Skip TLS certificate verification (for self-signed certificates)
remoteAgent.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
remoteAgent.pendingApprovalHint: Please approve this device on the OpenClaw Gateway
remoteAgent.pendingTimeout: Approval request expired (5 minutes). Please try again.

wcore.providerNote: Provider and API key settings are managed in the Models page. Wayland Core supports: Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock.

shared.restartRequired: This change requires a restart to take effect.

channelsIndex.title: Channels
channelsIndex.subtitle: Connect Wayland to messaging platforms. Each connection has its own setup, credentials, and status.
channelsIndex.smsTwilioTagline: Send and receive SMS via Twilio.
channelsIndex.msteamsTagline: Bot Framework via Azure Bot Service.
channelsIndex.twitchTagline: Twitch chat via IRC or EventSub.
channelsIndex.emailImapTagline: Bring your own IMAP / SMTP inbox.
channelsIndex.slackTagline: Post and receive messages in your Slack workspace.
channelsIndex.telegramTagline: Bot-based messaging via the Telegram Bot API.
channelsIndex.discordTagline: Bot integration for Discord servers and DMs.
channelsIndex.larkTagline: Workspace messaging for Lark / Feishu groups.
channelsIndex.wechatTagline: QR-based pairing with WeChat personal accounts.
channelsIndex.webhookTagline: Generic inbound webhook for custom integrations.
channelsIndex.emailTagline: Inbound & outbound email via IMAP / SMTP.
channelsIndex.channelsMovedHint: Channels have been moved to Integrations →
channelsIndex.detailComingSoon: Per-channel setup with credential fields and Connection Card UI lands in the next release.

modelsPage.title: Models
modelsPage.subtitle: Connect a provider with a key or a Google sign-in. Your models appear automatically.
modelsPage.loadError: Couldn't load your connected providers. Try again in a moment.

connect.title: Connect a provider
connect.keyPlaceholder: Paste a key from any provider…
connect.hintDefault: Wayland recognizes the provider on its own — no need to pick one.
connect.hintAmbiguous: Looks like an OpenAI-style key — connect to confirm the provider.
connect.cloudHint: this provider needs a credential form from Browse.
connect.googleFailed: Google sign-in failed. Please try again.
connect.errorUnauthorized: That {{provider}} key was rejected — unauthorized. Check the key and try again.
connect.errorNoCredit: Connected, but that {{provider}} key has no usable credit. Add billing to run models.
connect.errorOffline: Couldn't reach {{provider}} — you appear to be offline. Check your connection and retry.
connect.errorUnrecognized: That key was not recognized. Open Browse and pick your provider.
connect.errorCloud: Recognized {{provider}} — this provider needs more than one field. Open Browse to enter the full credentials.
connect.errorAmbiguous: Couldn't tell which provider this key is for. Open Browse and pick the provider.
connect.errorNoModels: {{provider}} connected but returned no models. Try refreshing it from Manage.
connect.errorUnknown: Couldn't connect to {{provider}}. Please try again.
connect.undecryptableHint: Your saved key for this provider is unreadable. Please re-enter it.

detected.found: Found a {{provider}} API key on this machine. Use it?

empty.note: No providers connected yet. Connect one above — the fastest start is <1>Continue with Google</1>.

manage.sectionExplain: Recommended models are on by default — the latest plus one generation back. Toggle any off, or switch on an older, image, or audio model. Search the whole catalog.
manage.loadError: Couldn't load this provider's models. Try again.
manage.empty: This provider returned no models. Refresh to try again.
manage.toggleFailed: Couldn't change that model. Please try again.
manage.refreshFailed: Couldn't refresh this provider. Please try again.
manage.disconnectBody: Disconnecting {{provider}} removes it and its models. You can reconnect later with a key.
manage.disconnectFailed: Couldn't disconnect this provider. Please try again.
manage.rekeyBody: Paste a new API key for this provider. It replaces the current credentials and runs a connection test.
manage.rekeyCloudDisabled: Cloud credentials are re-keyed from the cloud connection form, not here.
manage.errorUnauthorized: That {{provider}} key was rejected — unauthorized. Check the key and try again.
manage.errorNoCredit: That {{provider}} key has no usable credit. Add billing to run models.
manage.errorOffline: Couldn't reach {{provider}} — you appear to be offline. Check your connection and retry.
manage.errorUnrecognized: That key was not recognized for {{provider}}.
manage.errorNoModels: {{provider}} accepted the key but returned no models. Try refreshing.

browse.title: All providers
browse.subtitle: Pick one to connect. Most take a key; cloud providers take a short credential form.
browse.keySubtitle: Paste an API key for {{provider}} to connect.
browse.errorUnauthorized: That {{provider}} key was rejected — unauthorized. Check the key and try again.
browse.errorNoCredit: Connected, but that {{provider}} key has no usable credit. Add billing to run models.
browse.errorOffline: Couldn't reach {{provider}} — you appear to be offline. Check your connection and retry.
browse.errorUnrecognized: That key was not recognized for {{provider}}. Check the key and try again.
browse.errorNoModels: {{provider}} connected but returned no models. Try refreshing it from Manage.
browse.errorUnknown: Couldn't connect to {{provider}}. Please try again.

subtitle.aws-bedrock: Bedrock needs IAM credentials and a region.
subtitle.vertex: Vertex needs a project, a region, and a service-account key.
subtitle.azure: Azure OpenAI needs your resource endpoint and an API key.

cloud.errorUnauthorized: Those {{provider}} credentials were rejected — unauthorized. Check them and try again.
cloud.errorNoCredit: Connected, but {{provider}} has no usable credit. Add billing to run models.
cloud.errorOffline: Couldn't reach {{provider}} — you appear to be offline. Check your connection and retry.
cloud.errorUnrecognized: Those credentials are incomplete or were not accepted for {{provider}}. Check every field.
cloud.errorNoModels: {{provider}} connected but returned no models. Try refreshing it from Manage.
cloud.errorUnknown: Couldn't connect to {{provider}}. Please try again.

homePicker.empty: No models available for this agent yet. Connect a provider to add one.

mcpPage.title: MCP Servers
mcpPage.subtitle: Connect Wayland to external tool servers via Model Context Protocol. Add, configure, and sync MCP servers across your agents.

imageGenPage.title: Image Generation
imageGenPage.subtitle: Configure the built-in image generation tool: choose a default model and enable or disable the MCP server.
imageGenPage.mcpToggleDescription: When enabled, agents can generate and edit images using the configured model.
imageGenPage.toggleError: Failed to toggle image generation. Please try again.

voicePage.title: Voice
voicePage.subtitle: Speech-to-text and text-to-speech settings for the chat box. Toggle sections on to enable voice input and output.

generalPage.title: General
generalPage.subtitle: App-wide preferences: language, startup behaviour, timeouts, notifications, and storage paths.

notificationsPage.title: Notifications
notificationsPage.subtitle: When and how Wayland alerts you.
notificationsPage.comingSoon: Per-category toggles, quiet hours, and sound controls land in the next release.
notificationsPage.masterHelp: When off, Wayland sends no system notifications. Other rules below are ignored.
notificationsPage.agentFinishedHelp: Fires when a long-running task or team launcher completes.
notificationsPage.scheduledTaskHelp: Cron-style tasks notify when they run.
notificationsPage.channelMessageHelp: Telegram / Lark / DingTalk / WeChat messages from connected channels.

storagePage.title: Storage
storagePage.comingSoon: Usage breakdown, backup, restore, and sync land in the next release.
storagePage.clearConfirmBody: This permanently removes the files inside. Wayland will rebuild them as needed.
storagePage.syncComingSoon: End-to-end encrypted sync to a folder or relay.
storagePage.resetDescription: Permanently delete all conversations, settings, and cached data.
storagePage.resetConfirmBody: This permanently deletes all data including providers, conversations, and settings. This cannot be undone.

sync.title: Sync (E2EE)
sync.disabledDescription: End-to-end encrypted sync to a local folder. Keys never leave this device.
sync.disableConfirmBody: Stops syncing this device. Backup files in the chosen folder remain untouched.
sync.passphraseWarning: This passphrase encrypts your data. We can't recover it if you forget — write it down somewhere safe.
sync.passphraseTooShort: Passphrase must be at least {{min}} characters
sync.backendIntro: Choose where the encrypted bundle is stored.

editorPage.title: Editor
editorPage.comingSoon: Editor preferences are coming in the next release.
editorPage.defaultModeHelp: Which view to open new documents in.
editorPage.autoSaveDelayHelp: How long to wait after typing stops before saving.
editorPage.frontmatterHelp: Keep `--- ... ---` blocks at the top of Markdown files intact.
editorPage.syntaxHighlightHelp: Highlight code blocks in Markdown documents.

constitutionPage.subtitle: Wayland's rules. Loaded fresh on every turn — edits apply immediately, no restart. See §11 for how overrides work.
constitutionPage.resetConfirmBody: Your current edits will be lost. The default Constitution will be restored.
constitutionPage.tokenWarning: Approaching adherence ceiling (~2,000 tokens). Consider splitting into specialist overlays.
constitutionPage.tokenError: Past the adherence ceiling. Move sections into specialist overlays at ~/.wayland/specialists/<id>.md.

constitutionSpecialists.title: Specialist Overlays
constitutionSpecialists.description: Overlays add assistant-specific rules on top of the Constitution. They are opt-in by assistant ID and compose into that assistant's prompt.
constitutionSpecialists.idInvalid: Use only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores.
constitutionSpecialists.idDuplicate: An overlay with that ID already exists.
constitutionSpecialists.deleteConfirmBody: The overlay for "{{value}}" will be permanently removed.
constitutionSpecialists.empty: No specialist overlays yet. Add one to give a specific assistant extra rules.

agentsPage.title: Agents
agentsPage.subtitle: The assistants you chat with. Each one runs on a set of models.
agentsPage.empty: No agents detected yet. Wayland Core is always available once a model is connected.

about.wcore: Wayland’s own engine. Draws on every provider on your Models page.
about.claude: Anthropic’s coding agent. Signs in with its own account — it cannot run GPT or Gemini.
about.codex: OpenAI’s coding agent. Signs in with its own account — it cannot run Claude or Gemini.

flux.title: Flux Router
flux.desc: Lets any agent route across every model and provider you have connected — not just its own.
